info.name: LANAgent API Collection
info.description: LANAgent API v2.25.33 — Crypto strategy hardening pass: TokenTrader buy-path balance gating fix (executeTokenTrader was reading marketData.prices?.[network]?.nativeBalance — a field that doesn't exist; native balance lives at marketData.balances?.[network]?.native — and the price-impact-driven native switch was switching to native path without checking the wallet's actual BNB, so the trader dispatched 0.6 BNB swaps when the wallet had 0.09 BNB and the chain rejected with INSUFFICIENT_FUNDS; fix gates the switch on requiredNative + 0.005 gas reserve <= actualNative and falls back to stablecoin path with a 'Native path lower impact ... falling back to stablecoin path' warn line). TokenTrader trailing-stop display fix (state.trailingStopPrice was always set with pumpTrailingStopPercent 4.5% for both PUMP and MOON regimes but _analyzePump ignored that value and triggered on its own inline 12% wide stop, so status payloads showed a stop that never fired in PUMP — stored value now reflects regime-applicable stop: 12% PUMP, 4.5% MOON, 5% high-gain MOON, 8% standard; _analyzePump's inline calc unchanged so behavior preserved, only displayed value moves). LP MM fee accounting fix (lpManager.collectFeesV3 returned only {txHash, success} never the amounts; lpMarketMaker.collectFees updated lastFeeCollectAt but never incremented totalFeesCollectedSKYNET/WBNB — fix: collectFeesV3 now returns {txHash, success, amount0, amount1} with tokensOwed0/1 formatted to ether-units snapshot just before collect tx, collectFees reads those, increments lifetime totals in one saveState, logs +X SKYNET, +Y WBNB (lifetime: ...) per collection). Backfill probe via on-chain Collect events for the active LP MM position found 0 historical events (single mint, no Decrease, no Collect, tokensOwed=0, feeGrowthInside last=0) — '0/0' lifetime totals were factually correct, just for a different reason than the audit assumed. Sub-finding fixed in same session: rebalancePosition correctly updates state.tokenId on the new position but never refreshed openedAt so the displayed value stuck on the very first position's mint time even after the strategy rotated through new tokenIds — added openedAt: now to the saveState call alongside the new tokenId, with comments documenting why rebalanceCount (strategy-lifetime telemetry) and rebalancesLast24h (circuit-breaker rolling window at line 492) stay rolling — zeroing the latter would let the strategy spam-rebalance. Prod state corrected in-place via mongosh; PM2 restarted afterward so the in-memory state matches the DB row. Tooling left behind: scripts/backfill-lpmm-fees.js is committed and re-runnable, uses NodeReal's free public archive endpoint (50k-block range cap, 272 chunks in ~30s for a 70-day window), prints a paste-ready mongosh update if it ever finds non-zero amounts. v2.25.32 base — ALICE-authored capability-upgrade PR triage (#2116-#2126). 11 PRs reviewed: 1 merged as-is (#2121 ModelCache aggregation static — read-side correct, underlying trackUsage write-side is dead infrastructure but the method works the moment something starts calling it), 4 closed-and-replaced with manually-implemented working versions (#2118 cookiesAdmin actor identity in audit log lines using a small helper that handles both JWT and API-key auth modes; #2119 selfHealing schedule/listScheduled/cancelScheduled actions backed by the existing Agenda taskScheduler singleton with named 'self-healing-action' job persisted in MongoDB so schedules survive restarts; #2122 lpMarketMaker public /health endpoint mounted above the auth middleware so it actually works as a liveness probe and reports useful state via lpMarketMaker.getConfig; #2125 checkly schedule_check/list_scheduled_checks/cancel_scheduled_check actions polling the Checkly API on a recurring interval, also Agenda-backed), 6 closed without salvage (#2116 agentAccessor placeholder validation that would block agent boot, #2117 ContractABI projection that strips the abi field from getByAddressAndNetwork — the very field that defines the method's purpose, #2120 documentLoader conditional-GET branch unreachable behind cache-first early-return, #2123 UserPreference templates with placeholder data unrelated to any real plugin's preference shape, #2124 outputSchemas adding inert 'version' field with no version-aware logic — same defect as previously-closed #2113, #2126 metricsUpdater wraps a write operation in a 5-min result cache, defeating the entire purpose of the recurring metrics rebuild). Per-PR rationale on each closed PR. Same Agenda-backed scheduler pattern matches src/api/plugins/integromatnowmake.js. v2.25.31 base — Crypto token-trader heartbeat-manager auto-start fix and restore-state persistence fix. Two latent bugs surfaced when investigating a stale lastAnalysis timestamp on the SIREN token-trader: the per-token TokenTraderHeartbeatManager only ran .startAll() at agent init when allTokenTraders.size > 0, so any token configured at runtime via POST /api/crypto/strategy/token-trader/configure on a fresh registry left the manager with .started=false and ticks blocked at if (!this.started) return — the token only ran via the 10-min main-heartbeat fallback path instead of its regime-based interval (3 min during PUMP). And POST /api/crypto/strategy/token-trader/restore-state mutated tokenStrategy.state directly without calling persistRegistryState(), so a restored cost basis / realized PnL / trailing stop only lived in memory until the next full execute() cycle finally persisted it via the finally block. Fixed both in src/api/crypto.js: configure now calls tokenHeartbeatManager.startAll() if the manager exists but isn't started yet (picks up the newly-registered instance), and restore-state now persists the registry to MongoDB before responding. Caused by an earlier registry-rebuild incident (commit 2853417f restored the missing strategy registrations from the 2026-04-10 public→genesis sync, but didn't repopulate the runtime token_trader instance for SIREN — the secondaryStrategy field was reset to null and the tokenTraders map was empty after restart). Recovered SIREN live with two API calls (configure + restore-state) and the trader caught the in-progress SIREN pump from $0.75 to $1.024 (+37%) — three consecutive scale-out sells (level 20 / 25 / 30) during the rescue netted +$33.46 in 7 minutes, lifetime realized PnL $50.43 → $83.89. v2.25.30 base — ALICE-authored capability upgrades. 23 open self-modification PRs triaged one by one: 12 merged with corrections (added /api/avatar/health, added query-filter support to /api/avatar/gallery with owner / createdAfter / createdBefore plus 5-min cache and 3× retry, added /api/scammer-registry/report-history/:address with chronological event audit trail, added NetworkDevice lifecycleStatus + mark helpers, added BaseProvider.getDetailedTokenUsageStats, added Email.batchProcessEmails chunking + getEmailsByConversation pagination, added DevelopmentPlan.filterItems creationDateRange, added AutoAccount.archiveInactiveAccounts wired through Agenda daily 00:00, added selfModLock pre-expiration warning, made selfDiagnostics interval adaptive at startup, retry-wrapped gravatar fetch and BitNet provider, fixed systemReports cached-export to preserve Content-Type and not JSON-encode CSV); 11 closed with substantive comments explaining defects (placeholder code, hallucinated NASA endpoint /planetary/data — verified 404 via DEMO_KEY curl, security regression that would leak this.config credentials via external gateway, redundant globalLimiter, deletes-working-rate-limiter that calls non-existent service methods, ordering bug in adaptive circuit breaker, etc.). v2.25.29 base — ALICE auto-post lockout fix: Twitter/X and MindSwarm posting stopped 2026-05-01 (5-day silence) because the dedup filter in _gatherPostContext checked the last 8 posts against a catalog of only 7 topic categories, so once recent posts covered them all every candidate item got stripped (filteredItems.length === 0, hasContent: false, silent .debug() skip). Self-perpetuating because posting halt meant the recent-8-posts window never advanced. Fixed by expanding the topic catalog from 7 to 12 (added healing, skynetEcon, shipped, capabilities, providers — each pulling from data the agent already collects via selfHealing.getStatus, p2pFederation.getSkynetServiceStats, selfModification.getStats, apiManager.apis.size, providerManager.getProviderList) and converting the silent .debug() skip messages to .info() with diagnostic context (raw item count, post-dedup count, recent-topic matches, first 60 chars of each raw item) so future silent skips show their cause. SkynetAPIBot Telegraf handlerTimeout bumped from 90s default to 5 min so /dl downloads on slow extractors (Rumble via FlareSolverr, 60-180s) don't fire spurious 'unexpected error' replies mid-download. Persistent chat-action keepalive (upload_video / upload_voice / typing) re-sent every 4s so the indicator doesn't expire during 10-60s yt-dlp runs. Threaded replies via reply_to_message_id so bot responses don't float to the bottom of busy group chats. Status message updates as work progresses ('Fetching mp4 from youtube.com…' → 'Got Me_at_the_zoo.mp4 (614 KB), uploading…') and auto-deletes once the file lands. v2.25.28 base — External media-download fixes: /api/external/service/ytdlp/download was returning file.path: '[redacted]' (paying customers paid 3 cr, agent downloaded the file, but no fetchable URL came back) because the responseSanitizer correctly redacts /root/lanagent-deploy/downloads/<file> internal paths. Fixed in src/api/external/routes/plugins.js by minting a 60-min download token URL (mirroring /youtube/download) before sanitizer runs — applies to ytdlp/ffmpeg/imageTools/pdf when result.file.path or string file is present. /api/external/youtube/download was 502'ing with 'ytdlp.execute is not a function' because apiManager.apis.get('ytdlp') returns a wrapper { instance, enabled, ... } not the plugin instance — fixed in youtube.js by unwrapping .instance. v2.25.27 base — Gateway repo workflow + bundled gateway-side fixes (RequestLog coverage, agentName fix, mobile responsive admin, etc). 23 open self-modification PRs triaged one by one: 12 merged with corrections (added /api/avatar/health, added query-filter support to /api/avatar/gallery with owner / createdAfter / createdBefore plus 5-min cache and 3× retry, added /api/scammer-registry/report-history/:address with chronological event audit trail, added NetworkDevice lifecycleStatus + mark helpers, added BaseProvider.getDetailedTokenUsageStats, added Email.batchProcessEmails chunking + getEmailsByConversation pagination, added DevelopmentPlan.filterItems creationDateRange, added AutoAccount.archiveInactiveAccounts wired through Agenda daily 00:00, added selfModLock pre-expiration warning, made selfDiagnostics interval adaptive at startup, retry-wrapped gravatar fetch and BitNet provider, fixed systemReports cached-export to preserve Content-Type and not JSON-encode CSV); 11 closed with substantive comments explaining defects (placeholder code, hallucinated NASA endpoint /planetary/data — verified 404 via DEMO_KEY curl, security regression that would leak this.config credentials via external gateway, redundant globalLimiter, deletes-working-rate-limiter that calls non-existent service methods, ordering bug in adaptive circuit breaker, etc.). v2.25.29 base — ALICE auto-post lockout fix: Twitter/X and MindSwarm posting stopped 2026-05-01 (5-day silence) because the dedup filter in _gatherPostContext checked the last 8 posts against a catalog of only 7 topic categories, so once recent posts covered them all every candidate item got stripped (filteredItems.length === 0, hasContent: false, silent .debug() skip). Self-perpetuating because posting halt meant the recent-8-posts window never advanced. Fixed by expanding the topic catalog from 7 to 12 (added healing, skynetEcon, shipped, capabilities, providers — each pulling from data the agent already collects via selfHealing.getStatus, p2pFederation.getSkynetServiceStats, selfModification.getStats, apiManager.apis.size, providerManager.getProviderList) and converting the silent .debug() skip messages to .info() with diagnostic context (raw item count, post-dedup count, recent-topic matches, first 60 chars of each raw item) so future silent skips show their cause. SkynetAPIBot Telegraf handlerTimeout bumped from 90s default to 5 min so /dl downloads on slow extractors (Rumble via FlareSolverr, 60-180s) don't fire spurious 'unexpected error' replies mid-download. Persistent chat-action keepalive (upload_video / upload_voice / typing) re-sent every 4s so the indicator doesn't expire during 10-60s yt-dlp runs. Threaded replies via reply_to_message_id so bot responses don't float to the bottom of busy group chats. Status message updates as work progresses ('Fetching mp4 from youtube.com…' → 'Got Me_at_the_zoo.mp4 (614 KB), uploading…') and auto-deletes once the file lands. v2.25.28 base — External media-download fixes: /api/external/service/ytdlp/download was returning file.path: '[redacted]' (paying customers paid 3 cr, agent downloaded the file, but no fetchable URL came back) because the responseSanitizer correctly redacts /root/lanagent-deploy/downloads/<file> internal paths. Fixed in src/api/external/routes/plugins.js by minting a 60-min download token URL (mirroring /youtube/download) before sanitizer runs — applies to ytdlp/ffmpeg/imageTools/pdf when result.file.path or string file is present. /api/external/youtube/download was 502'ing with 'ytdlp.execute is not a function' because apiManager.apis.get('ytdlp') returns a wrapper { instance, enabled, ... } not the plugin instance — fixed in youtube.js by unwrapping .instance. v2.25.27 base — Gateway repo workflow + bundled gateway-side fixes (RequestLog coverage, agentName fix, mobile responsive admin, etc).

request.description: Schedule a Zap to run once at a future ISO 8601 timestamp. Backed by the new `zapier-run-zap` Agenda job. Past times and invalid ISO strings are rejected. The job persists across restarts.
Manually trigger the same operation that the `archive-old-dev-items` Agenda job runs daily at 02:30. Archives DevelopmentPlan items with status='completed' and completedAt older than `days` days.
List configured notifications for any *arr service (radarr, sonarr, lidarr, readarr, prowlarr). Returns notification names, implementations, and event trigger settings.
Create a new notification in any *arr service. Requires name and implementation. Optional: onGrab, onDownload, onUpgrade, onHealthIssue and implementation-specific fields.
Delete a notification from any *arr service by its ID. Get IDs from get_notifications.
Serve agent avatar image. No authentication required. Returns image with correct Content-Type.
Upload new avatar (base64 JSON, max 5MB) or update description only (omit image field). Supports PNG, JPG, GIF, WebP.
Get agent identity card: name, version, avatar URL, description, personality, plugin count, capabilities, interfaces.

Get ERC-8004 agent identity status: chain, agentId, staleness detection, gas estimate, registry address, explorer URL. Auto-recovers from on-chain data if DB state is lost (queries ownerOf, tokenURI, getAgentWallet on the Identity Registry).
Generate and preview the ERC-8004 registration file JSON. Includes agent name, version, avatar, capabilities, interfaces, and endpoints.
Upload avatar and registration file to IPFS via Pinata, then mint ERC-8004 identity NFT on-chain. Returns agentId, txHash, explorerUrl.
Re-generate registration file, re-upload to IPFS, and call setAgentURI() on-chain to update the identity.
Save Pinata IPFS API keys (encrypted in database). Required for IPFS uploads before minting.
Check if Pinata IPFS API keys are configured. Returns configured (bool) and source.
Link wallet to Agent Identity via EIP-712 signed setAgentWallet() on-chain call. Optionally pass walletAddress in body to link a different wallet.
Sync agent avatar to external services (Gravatar). Auto-crops non-square images. Uses OAuth token if connected.
Start Gravatar OAuth2 flow. Returns authorizeUrl to open in browser for WordPress.com authorization.
Check Gravatar OAuth connection status. Returns connected (bool), source, hasClientId.
Disconnect Gravatar OAuth. Removes stored token from database.

item.name: Mediastack - Search News with Date Range

request.description: Search news with date range filtering. Uses dateFrom and dateTo parameters (YYYY-MM-DD format).
Search for media across all Jellyfin libraries.
List all media libraries configured in Jellyfin.
Get all active Jellyfin playback sessions (who is watching what).
Browse media items in Jellyfin with type filters (Movie, Series, Audio, MusicAlbum).
Get Jellyfin server information including version, OS, and architecture.
Search for books in the Calibre library by title, author, tag, or any field.
Get all books by a specific author from Calibre.
List all available Calibre libraries.
Get Calibre library statistics including total books, authors, tags, and series counts.
Get a download URL for a book in a specific format (EPUB, PDF, MOBI, etc.).
Get the most recently added books in the Calibre library.
Health check endpoint - No authentication required, exempt from rate limiting
Get git hosting provider settings (GitHub or GitLab)
Update git hosting provider settings. Set provider to 'github' or 'gitlab'

query.description: Maximum number of versions to return (default 50, max 200)

request.description: Enable or disable auto-approve for all feature requests. When enabled, existing pending requests are bulk-approved.
Get wake word training status and model info. Shows if custom model exists, training progress, and sample counts.
Returns instructions for starting wake word training via Telegram. Training is done by sending voice messages to collect samples.
Get the current status of voice interaction (wake word listening). Shows if enabled, listening state, audio device, and wake word configuration.
Start wake word listening. The server microphone will listen for the configured wake word. State is persisted to database and survives restarts.
Stop wake word listening. State is persisted to database - voice interaction will remain disabled on restart.

item.name: Media Generation
item.description: AI-powered image and video generation via OpenAI and HuggingFace

body.raw:
{
  "plugin": "prReviewer",
  "action": "updateSettings",
  "enabled": true,
  "schedule": "0 9,21 * * *",
  "aiProvider": "anthropic",
  "aiModel": "claude-3-5-opus-20241022",
  "autoMerge": true,
  "autoImplement": true,
  "deployAfterMerge": true,
  "rollbackOnFailure": true,
  "commentOnPRs": true,
  "maxPRsPerRun": 10
}

request.description: Check Dry.AI authentication status
Automated Dry.AI auth: register, read verification email, verify, save token
Register or login to Dry.AI with email
Verify email with code from Dry.AI registration
List all Dry.AI spaces/smartspaces
Create an AI-powered app space with custom types and interactive pages
Create a new smartspace in Dry.AI
Create a new item in Dry.AI using natural language
Create a custom type/schema in a Dry.AI space
Create a folder in a Dry.AI space
Batch import multiple items into a Dry.AI folder
Search Dry.AI data across spaces
List items in a Dry.AI folder
Get details of a specific Dry.AI item
Update a Dry.AI item using natural language
Update a type/schema in Dry.AI
Update a Dry.AI smartspace
Update a folder in Dry.AI
Bulk update items in a Dry.AI folder
Share a Dry.AI item
Delete an item from Dry.AI
Delete Dry.AI items matching a search query
Get help and documentation about Dry.AI features
Run a multi-intent natural language prompt against Dry.AI data
Generate a structured report from Dry.AI data
Remove stored Dry.AI credentials
Send a formatted message using Slack Block Kit
Compare improvement stats between current and previous N-day periods. Returns totals, merged counts, averages, percentage change, and trend direction.
Process multiple NumbersAPI fact requests in parallel. Each request can be trivia, math, date, year, or random. Returns consolidated results array.
Analyze text to determine if it was AI-generated. Returns verdict (human/ai_generated/uncertain), confidence (0-1), and reasoning.
Analyze an image to determine if it was AI-generated. Uses ViT deep-fake detection model.
Analyze audio to detect AI-generated speech. Transcribes via Whisper then analyzes transcript.
Analyze a video for AI-generated frames. Extracts frames via FFmpeg and runs per-frame analysis.
Auto-detect content type (text, image URL, audio URL, video URL) and analyze for AI generation.
Validate multiple phone numbers in a single batch (max 50). Returns per-number results with aggregate success/failure counts.
List all accounts. Supports query params: status, serviceName, tags, startDate, endDate
List accounts filtered by creation date range
Register multiple accounts at once
Update status of multiple accounts at once
Set device color using color names (red, green, blue, white, yellow, purple, orange, etc.) including compound colors like 'bright white', 'warm white', 'dark blue'
Set device brightness using percentage. Accepts both 'level' (1-100) and 'brightness' ('50%', 'fifty percent', etc.) parameters
Apply predefined themes: relax, party, movie, romance, energize, sleep, focus, nature
Subscribe to real-time device state updates via MQTT
Control devices by name instead of MAC address when fromAI=true
Create a schedule to turn on device at 100% brightness with white color

item.name: Crypto Strategy
item.description: Autonomous multi-strategy trading with market regime detection. Dollar Maximizer (primary) uses 72h trend analysis to dynamically adjust buy thresholds. Token Trader (secondary) for speculative tokens with grid trading. Supports Ethereum, BSC, Polygon with per-asset thresholds, gas reserve protection, and V2/V3 DEX routing.

request.description: Get strategy status including positions, price baselines, and configuration
Enable the autonomous trading strategy
Disable the autonomous trading strategy
Manually trigger a strategy analysis loop
Get current strategy configuration
Update strategy configuration including price thresholds
Set network mode to testnet or mainnet
Emergency kill switch - stops all trading immediately
Clear emergency stop and resume trading
View decision history with market snapshots and reasoning
Combined portfolio: native tokens, stablecoins, token trader positions with balances, prices, 24h change
Wallet counterparty interactions for graph visualization. Returns addresses, interaction counts, contract flags, USD values, and agent ENS name.
Get swap quote before executing (BNB to BUSD example)
Execute token swap via DEX (BNB to BUSD example)
Unwrap WETH/WBNB to native ETH/BNB. Validates balance before executing.
List all supported networks for DEX swaps
Get list of networks disabled for dollar maximizer trading. Returns array of disabled network names.
Set which networks are disabled for dollar maximizer trading. Valid networks: ethereum, bsc. Settings persist in MongoDB across restarts.
Check whether automatic scam token reporting to the on-chain registry is enabled. Defaults to enabled.

Enable or disable automatic scam token reporting. When enabled, the agent reports confirmed scam tokens (confidence >= 50, requiring 2+ signals) to the on-chain scammer registry after each sweep cycle. Settings persist in MongoDB.
Get all current strategy positions
Get ERC-20 token balance for the agent wallet. Returns formatted balance, raw amount, and decimals. Example: SKYNET balance on BSC.
Update position state for a network
Pre-populate 24h price history from CoinGecko for volatility strategies. Eliminates warmup period.
Dynamically adjust maxTradePercentage and slippageTolerance based on current market volatility regime
Set the schedule interval for the crypto strategy agent. Creates/updates an Agenda job.
Get current schedule info including interval, next run time, and last run time
Initialize token scanner with wallet address
Get token scanner status
Start periodic token scanning
Stop periodic token scanning
Trigger manual scan of all networks
Get all detected tokens
Get safe sellable tokens
Sell all safe non-stablecoin tokens via chunked progressive sell (100% → 0.01%) with 99% slippage for fee-on-transfer tokens
Get detected scam tokens
Check if specific token is safe
Get platform capabilities including version, available indicators (50+ across 6 categories), condition operators, and action types for rule-based strategies
Export a single strategy to Universal Strategy Format (.strategy.json). Replace 'dollar_maximizer' with any strategy name.
Export all strategies as a bundle with metadata
Validate a strategy file without importing. Returns validation status, warnings, and compatibility info.
Import a strategy from Universal Strategy Format. Use mode 'merge' to add/update or 'replace' to overwrite existing.

Returns all active token trader instances. Use ?token=ADDRESS to get a specific instance. Response includes activeInstances count and per-token status with regime, position, P&L, tracking, and settings.

Adds a new token trader instance (or updates existing). Persists to DB immediately so tokens survive restarts. Reconfiguring the same token preserves position data. Multiple tokens can trade simultaneously with independent funds and regimes. Capital allocation is per-instance and sums for total secondary allocation.
Update token trader config (capitalAllocationPercent, dumpThreshold, tokenTaxPercent, etc.) WITHOUT resetting state, PnL, or regime. Safe for live position changes.
Restore state for a specific token trader instance. Requires ?token=ADDRESS or tokenAddress in body.

query.description: Optional: exit specific token. Omit to exit all.

request.description: Exit specific token position (?token=ADDRESS) or all positions. Sells tokens, removes instance from registry, cleans up state.
Get arbitrage scan tokens grouped by source (default, tokenTrader, custom) along with current config and scan status.
Add a custom token to the arbitrage scan list. Default and token trader tokens cannot be modified.
Remove a custom token from the arbitrage scan list. Only custom tokens can be removed.
Get arbitrage scanner status including recent opportunities, total PnL, and current configuration.

Get daily P&L history for charting. Returns cumulative P&L calibrated against actual token trader lifetime P&L. Data sourced from DailyPnL MongoDB collection (backfilled from trade logs, updated live every 15 minutes). Defaults to last 30 days if no dates specified.
Get current LP Market Maker status, position info, and configuration.
Enable LP Market Maker with optional config overrides.
Close active position and disable LP Market Maker.
Open a new V3 concentrated liquidity position.
Close the active LP position.
Manually rebalance the LP position.
Collect accumulated LP fees.
Get MQTT service and Event Engine status
List all configured MQTT brokers
Create or update an MQTT broker configuration
Enable or disable a broker
List all discovered MQTT devices
Send command to an MQTT device
Get all topic states with pagination
Publish a message to an MQTT topic
List all automation rules
Create a new automation rule
Manually trigger an automation rule for testing
Get message history for a topic with optional aggregation
Get current UPS status including battery, load, and runtime
List all configured UPS devices
Get power event history (on_battery, power_restored, etc.)
Get UPS monitoring configuration
Update UPS monitoring settings
Acknowledge a power event
Scan for nearby Bluetooth devices
List all known Bluetooth devices
List paired Bluetooth devices
List currently connected Bluetooth devices
Pair with a Bluetooth device
Remove device pairing
Connect to a paired Bluetooth device
Disconnect from a Bluetooth device
Trust device for auto-connect
Get detailed info about a Bluetooth device
Send Wake-on-LAN magic packet to wake a device
Wake a device by its name or alias
List devices that can be woken via WOL

Move a Trello card from one list to another

Returns the current MindSwarm ad configuration: ad_enabled, ad_free_mode, ad_daily_rate (per cryptocurrency), ad_platform_wallets (per cryptocurrency), ad_max_duration_days, ad_auto_approve. Use this to decide whether createAd will take the free or paid path.

item.name: Create Ad — auto-generated image (free or paid)

body.raw:
{
  "plugin": "mindswarm",
  "action": "createAd",
  "title": "My service",
  "description": "What it does in <= 280 chars.",
  "linkUrl": "https://example.com",
  "duration": 7,
  "placement": "inline",
  "imagePrompt": "Clean modern banner showing the product",
  "preferCrypto": "bnb"
}

request.description: End-to-end ad creation. Resolves the image (imagePrompt → imageGenerationService → uploadMedia), submits the ad, reads /ads/settings, then either (free mode: ad_free_mode=true or daily_rate=0) calls /pay with empty body or (paid mode) sends native crypto via transactionService.sendNative and submits the txHash. preferCrypto picks which native rate to use for paid mode (eth/bnb/matic). Returns { stage: 'pending_review'|'active'|'payment_submitted', ad, imageUrl, txHash?, amount? }.

item.name: Create Ad — supply image URL directly

request.description: Same orchestration as auto-generated, but skips the imageGen + upload steps.

Wraps PATCH /api/ads/:adId. Only the fields supplied are sent; everything else is preserved. _id, impressions, clicks, payment history survive the edit. imageUrl is NOT patchable — use cancel + recreate for image swaps. Real field changes on approved/active ads re-run approval (instant under ad_auto_approve, otherwise drops to pending_review).

Direct pay call. In free mode pass just { plugin, action, adId } — server ignores payment data and activates immediately. Normally you'd use createAd which orchestrates submit + pay; this is here for manual retries.
Use this for image swaps (imageUrl is not patchable) — cancel the ad, then call createAd again with the new image. Forfeits impressions/clicks; for everything else editAd is preferred.

Execute code in an isolated Docker container. Supported languages: python, node, bash, ruby, go, php, java, rust, c, cpp, typescript, perl, kotlin (14 total). Returns stdout, stderr, exitCode. Requires ERC-8004 agent identity + 0.002 BNB payment.
Merge 2-20 PDF files into a single document. Returns a download token. Requires ERC-8004 agent identity + 0.0005 BNB payment.
Split a PDF by extracting specific page ranges. Returns a download token. Requires ERC-8004 agent identity + 0.0005 BNB payment.

Compress a PDF using Ghostscript. Quality options: screen (smallest), ebook (balanced), printer (highest). Returns download token with size comparison. Requires ERC-8004 agent identity + 0.0005 BNB payment.
Add a text watermark to all pages of a PDF. Positions: center, top-left, top-right, bottom-left, bottom-right. Returns download token. Requires ERC-8004 agent identity + 0.0005 BNB payment.

Extract text and metadata from a PDF. Format options: text (plain text + page count) or json (text + metadata including title, author, creator, dates). Returns inline JSON. Requires ERC-8004 agent identity + 0.0005 BNB payment.
Process a single document (OCR or structured data extraction). Operations: ocr, extract. Languages: eng, etc. Output formats: json, text. Requires ERC-8004 agent identity + payment.
Process up to 10 documents in parallel. Returns aggregated results with per-file success/error. Uses safePromiseAll for fault-tolerant batch processing. Requires ERC-8004 agent identity + payment.

Scrape a URL and extract content. Tiers: basic (1 credit), stealth (2, forces Puppeteer), full (3, +HTML), render (3, +HTML+screenshot+FlareSolverr). v2.25.25: render dropped from 5 to 3 to match full. Response includes ogImage (validated, ad-filtered). extractType: text (default) or structured. Optional: selectors, userAgent.
Scrape with stealth tier (2 credits) — forces Puppeteer rendering for Cloudflare-protected or ad-heavy sites. Returns clean og:title, validated og:image, and ad-filtered image list.
Health check endpoint to verify scraping service availability.
Generate an AI image with optional format conversion. Supported formats: png (default), jpeg, webp, tiff. Uses sharp for format conversion. Requires ERC-8004 agent identity + payment.

Ask ALICE about her external service gateway stats via NLP. Returns revenue in BNB, payment count, service list with pricing and request counts, and recent payments. Other valid commands: 'how much revenue from external services', 'what payments have you received', 'how much BNB have you earned'.

Ask ALICE about SKYNET token and the Skynet P2P network. Uses intent 115 (skynetInfo) — answers from built-in system prompt knowledge. Other valid queries: 'do I need SKYNET tokens?', 'how does the P2P network work?', 'tell me about bounties', 'what is skynet governance?', 'how do trust scores work?'.

Get live Skynet P2P network status via NLP. Uses intent 116 (skynetNetworkStatus) — queries P2PPeer, SkynetServiceConfig, SkynetBounty, SkynetGovernance models. Returns peer count, online/trusted peers, service catalog size, open bounties, active proposals. Other valid queries: 'how many peers are connected?', 'show P2P services', 'skynet peer list'.

Get live SKYNET token allocation and payment data via NLP. Uses intent 117 (skynetTokenInfo) — queries SkynetTokenLedger and SkynetPayment models. Returns contract address, allocation breakdown (LP/staking/bounty/treasury/reserve/bought), total minted/bought/tradeable, and recent payment history. Other valid queries: 'SKYNET allocations', 'how many SKYNET in bounty pool?', 'SKYNET contract address'.
Get Skynet connection status including identity fingerprint, connection state, peers online, uptime, and message statistics.
Get Skynet settings including enabled state, registry URL, display name, auto-share and auto-install preferences, and identity info.

Save Skynet settings. Registry URL changes update .env and require restart. Preference changes (displayName, autoShare, autoInstallTrusted) take effect immediately. Use POST /p2p/api/toggle for enable/disable.
Enable or disable Skynet dynamically without restart. Persists to SystemSettings and starts/stops the P2P service immediately.
List all known peers with fingerprint, trust level, online status, capabilities count, transfer count, and last seen timestamp.
List only currently online peers.
Get detailed info for a specific peer including their capabilities list.
Set trust level for a peer. 'trusted' enables auto-install of plugins from this peer. 'untrusted' requires manual approval.
Send an encrypted ping to a peer to check latency.
Request the list of available plugins from a peer.
Request a specific plugin from a peer. The plugin will be sanitized, chunked, transferred, and queued for approval (unless peer is trusted).
Get plugin transfer history with direction, status, size, signature verification, and timestamps.
Get plugins awaiting user approval with manifest details, signature verification status, and SHA-256 hash.
Approve a pending plugin transfer and install it.
Reject a pending plugin transfer and discard the source code.
List all knowledge packs with status, direction, manifest, and import results.
Get knowledge packs awaiting user approval or AI evaluation.
Get full details of a specific knowledge pack.
Create a knowledge pack from existing memories matching the query filters.
Approve a pending knowledge pack and import its memories into the agent's memory system.
Reject a pending knowledge pack and discard its content.
Delete a local or rejected/failed knowledge pack.
Request knowledge pack list from a peer. Returns cached list if available.
Request a full knowledge pack transfer from a peer via chunked P2P protocol.
Get Skynet revenue statistics including total revenue, payment count, and service stats.
List all available Skynet services with their enabled status and SKYNET pricing.
Enable/disable a Skynet service and set its SKYNET token price.
Enable or disable all Skynet services at once.
Discover available services from local plugins and create SkynetServiceConfig entries.
List recent SKYNET token payments received for services.
Get economy overview including bounty and governance statistics.
List all SKYNET bounties.
Create a new bounty with SKYNET token reward.
List all governance proposals.
Create a new governance proposal for token-weighted voting.
Vote on a governance proposal. Options: for, against, abstain.
Get current user's staking position including staked amount, pending rewards, APY, wallet balance, reward rate, and epoch timing.
Get contract-wide staking stats including total staked, reward rate, APY, remaining rewards, and epoch end time.
Stake SKYNET tokens into the staking contract. Auto-approves token spending if needed.
Unstake SKYNET tokens. Automatically claims any pending rewards.
Claim pending staking rewards. Returns claimedAmount and txHash. Logged to HistoricalTransaction for staking history. Agent auto-claims every 6h when > 1,000 SKYNET.
Fund a new reward epoch with SKYNET tokens. Owner only. Duration in seconds (default 604800 = 7 days).
Get staking transaction history. Returns HistoricalTransaction records: stakingClaim, stakingFund, feeDebit events with txHash, amount, date. Supports ?limit= query param.
Get fee-to-staking routing settings and current registry fee balance from the ledger. Returns enabled state, threshold, routing percent, epoch duration, and available fee balance.

Update fee-to-staking routing configuration. All fields optional. enabled: toggle routing on/off. threshold: minimum SKYNET fee balance to trigger (default 500000). percent: percentage of fee balance to route (default 100). epochDuration: staking reward epoch length in seconds (default 604800 = 7 days, minimum 86400 = 1 day).
Get ENS configuration status including base name, expiry, subnames, resolved address, and auto-renewal setting.
Check if a .eth name is available for registration. Returns availability and pricing (cost per year in ETH).
Check the expiry date of a registered .eth name. Returns expiry timestamp and days until expiry.
Step 1 of ENS registration: submit a commitment hash on-chain. Wait 60+ seconds before calling register. Returns commit tx hash and ready-at timestamp.
Step 2 of ENS registration: complete the registration after the 60s commit wait. Sends ETH payment and registers the name. Sets resolver and reverse record automatically.
Create a subname under the base ENS name (e.g., alice.lanagent.eth). Owner defaults to agent wallet if not specified. Sets address record on the subname.
Set reverse resolution so that looking up the wallet address returns the specified ENS name.
Renew a .eth name registration. Name defaults to base name if not specified. Sends ETH payment for the renewal period.
Update ENS settings. Supports auto-renewal toggle (daily check, renews when <30 days until expiry) and subname pricing for P2P provisioning (SKYNET tokens, 0 = free).

Request an ENS subname from the genesis peer via P2P network. The label becomes {label}.lanagent.eth. If the name is already taken, the system auto-retries with a fingerprint suffix. If payment is required and fails, the request is saved and retried daily. Also available via NLP: 'get me an ENS subname' or 'request subname coolbot'.
List all email leases. Optional status filter: active, pending, expired, revoked.
Get email lease statistics: active count, total count, expiring soon, total SKYNET revenue.
Get email lease pricing and settings: leasePrice, renewalPrice, leaseDurationDays, defaultQuotaMB, maxLeasesPerPeer, enabled status.
Update email lease pricing and settings. All fields optional.
Revoke an email lease. Deletes the mail account and notifies the peer via P2P.
Reset the password for a leased email account. New credentials are delivered to the peer via encrypted P2P channel.
List all tracked LP positions.
Refresh all LP position data from on-chain.
Get LP pair info including reserves, total supply, LP balance, and pool share.
Add liquidity to a V2 pair.
Remove a percentage of liquidity from a V2 pair.
Get V3 market maker config and state (position, ticks, fees, rebalance count).
Enable V3 market maker with optional config overrides (rangePercent, feeTier, allocationBNB, allocationSKYNET).
Close active V3 position and disable market maker.
Open new V3 concentrated liquidity position (creates pool if needed, wraps BNB to WBNB).
Remove all liquidity from active V3 position and deactivate.
Manual rebalance — removes liquidity and re-adds at current price center.
Collect accumulated V3 trading fees from active position.
Get scammer registry statistics including total flagged addresses, report counts, and fee information.
Check if a specific address is flagged in the scammer registry.

Chronological audit trail of every ScammerRegistered event targeting this address (reporter, category, evidence, block + tx hash, timestamp). Useful for revoke-and-re-flag scenarios; getReport() only shows current state.
Check if an address has immunity from being flagged based on token holdings threshold.
List flagged scammer addresses with pagination support.
Get the list of scam categories used for classifying reports.
Report an address as a scammer with category, evidence transaction hash, and reason.
Submit multiple scammer reports in a single batch transaction.
Remove a scammer flag from an address.

Set the fee required to submit a scammer report. Genesis-agent-only (onlyOwner on the diamond). Note: this endpoint was broken prior to v2.24.3 due to a missing ABI entry. Also note that the genesis instance auto-prices this value to a USD target hourly via the skynet-auto-price-fee Agenda job — manual updates here will be overwritten on the next run if they drift >25% from the USD target.

Set the token holding threshold that grants immunity from being flagged. Genesis-agent-only (onlyOwner on the diamond). Note: this endpoint was broken prior to v2.24.3 due to a missing ABI entry. The genesis instance can auto-price this value via the skynet-auto-price-immunity Agenda job (off by default at launch due to SKYNET price conditions — re-enabled via skynet.immunityThreshold.autoPrice SystemSettings key).
Get local scammer cache statistics (size, last sync time, staleness)
Check if an address is flagged in the scammer registry via natural language.
Get scammer registry statistics via natural language.
Report an address as a scammer via natural language.
List available coordination types
Get active/pending coordination intents
Past coordinations with optional status/type query params
Coordination statistics: success rate, counts by type
Propose a new coordination intent
Accept a coordination intent (use intent hash from propose)
Execute a ready coordination
Cancel a proposed coordination
Create 3D avatar from text prompt (or multipart photo upload)

query.description: ISO 8601 datetime — only return avatars created at or after this time (optional)
ISO 8601 datetime — only return avatars created at or before this time (optional)

request.description: List avatars with optional filters. Results cached 5 min server-side; underlying lookup retries 3× on transient failures.
Liveness probe for the avatar service. Returns { success: true, message: 'Avatar service is healthy' } when the router is mounted.
Avatar creation statistics
Get the active VRM avatar model ID (public, no auth required)
Set the active VRM avatar model (persists in MongoDB)
Get avatar details by ID
Update avatar customizations
Export avatar model (GLB format)
Mint avatar as ERC-721 NFT on BSC
Available and unlocked cosmetic items
Unlock a cosmetic item
Serve raw GLB binary for Three.js GLTFLoader. Returns Content-Type: model/gltf-binary. Accepts ?token= query param for auth.
Auto-rig avatar mesh with Blender headless. Creates 19-bone humanoid armature with automatic weight painting. Takes 10-30 seconds.
Rename an avatar (max 100 characters)
Delete avatar and all associated files (model, thumbnail, photo)
List available job types and pricing
Create job with BNB escrow
Check job status
Fund a created job
Get job deliverable after completion
Get trust level for an ENS agent
Find trust path between agents
Submit trust attestation
Full trust graph (admin)
Set trust level (admin)
Trust registry statistics (admin)
Agent oracle capabilities and supported domains
Oracle participation statistics
Active oracle requests (admin)
Past oracle participations (admin)
Pause oracle participation (admin)
Resume oracle participation (admin)

header.description: Shared secret matched against process.env.AGENT_ADMIN_KEY on the agent. 503 if unset on agent; 401 on mismatch.
Shared secret matched against process.env.AGENT_ADMIN_KEY on the agent.

request.description: Compress and optimize an image with optional format conversion. Returns base64 data URI.
Resize image to target dimensions. Fit modes: cover, contain, fill, inside, outside.
Smart crop using attention-based center detection. Also supports manual region crop with left/top/width/height.
Add text watermark. Positions: top-left, top-center, top-right, center, bottom-left, bottom-center, bottom-right.
Chain multiple operations in one call (max 10). Available ops: resize, crop, rotate, flip, flop, sharpen, blur, grayscale, negate, tint, trim, flatten, format, stripMetadata.
Get image dimensions, format, color space, channels, EXIF presence, file size.
Quick honeypot detection for an ERC20 token via GoPlus API
0-100 safety score for an ERC20 token
Full token audit including honeypot, tax, ownership, liquidity, holders
Top holder concentration and distribution analysis
Full wallet profile with balance, age, tx count, risk flags
0-100 wallet risk score with weighted flags
List ERC20 token holdings for a wallet
Full Solidity security audit with findings by severity
Fast critical/high severity check only
Plain-English explanation of what a contract does
Generate challenge questions with answers included (single request)
Generate questions with token for server-side verification
Verify answers against a challenge token
List available question types and configuration

item.name: VPN Management
item.description: Dual-provider VPN management: WireGuard (inbound tunnel for api.lanagent.net) + ExpressVPN (outbound privacy). Both run simultaneously with wg0.conf PostUp hooks managing coexistence.

request.description: Get status of both VPN providers. Returns expressvpn object (connected, location, protocol, publicIP) and wireguard object (interfaceUp, handshake, transfer, peerReachable, pingMs, healthy).
WireGuard tunnel status: interface up/down, last handshake age, transfer stats, peer endpoint, peer reachability (ping), and healthy boolean.
Restart WireGuard tunnel (wg-quick down/up wg0). PostUp hooks in wg0.conf re-add the static route and iptables exception for ExpressVPN coexistence automatically.
Health check with auto-recovery. Bounces tunnel if handshake stale (>maxHandshakeAge seconds, default 180) or peer unreachable.
Connect ExpressVPN to a specific location. Pass empty location for smart location selection.
Disconnect ExpressVPN. Blocked when auto-connect is enabled unless force flag is set.
Smart connect by purpose: security (Switzerland, Iceland, Sweden), streaming (US, UK), speed (closest servers).
List available ExpressVPN server locations. Cached for 10 minutes.
Test ExpressVPN connectivity: ping multiple sites, check DNS, simple speed test.
Run diagnostics on both VPN providers: ExpressVPN installation/service, WireGuard tunnel health, network connectivity, DNS, firewall.
